title: Properties of shapes
description: Identify and compare shapes based on their attributes.  Shapes include triangle types, quadrilateral types, pentagons, and hexagons.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following shapes have $4$ right angles?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
A square is a quadrilateral with $4$ sides of equal length and $\blueE{4 \text{ right angles}}$.

A rectangle is a quadrilateral with $4$ sides and $\blueE{4 \text{ right angles}}$.

A right triangle is a triangle with $3$ sides and $\blueE{1 \text{ right angle}}$.

The following shapes have $4$ right angles:
 
 - Square
 - Rectangle

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following shapes are polygons with more than $4$ sides?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: A $\blueE{\text{polygon}}$ is a simple, closed figure with $\blueE{3 \text{ or more straight sides}}$.
A heart is **not** a polygon, because it has $\blueE{\text{curved sides}}$.

So then, which polygons have $\tealE{\text{more than 4 sides}}$?  

A triangle is a polygon with  $\maroonE{3}$ sides.

A rectangle is a polygon with  $\maroonE4$ sides. 

A pentagon is a polygon with $\tealE{5}$ sides.

A hexagon is a polygon with  $\tealE{6}$ sides.

The following shapes are polygons with more than $4$ sides:
 
 - Pentagon
 - Hexagon

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following shapes are polygons with $4$ sides or less?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: A $\blueE{\text{polygon}}$ is a simple, closed figure with $\blueE{3 \text{ or more straight sides}}$.

So then, which polygons have $\tealE{\text{4 sides or less}}$?  

A triangle is a polygon with  $\tealE{3}$ sides.

A rectangle is a polygon with  $\tealE4$ sides. 

A trapezoid is a polygon with  $\tealE4$ sides. 

A pentagon is a polygon with $\maroonE{5}$ sides.

A hexagon is a polygon with  $\maroonE{6}$ sides.

The following shapes are polygons with $4$ sides or less:
 
 - Triangle
 - Rectangle
 - Trapezoid

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following shapes are quadrilaterals?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: A $\maroonE{\text{quadrilateral}}$ is a polygon with $\maroonE4$ sides.

A right triangle has $3$ sides.

A square has $\maroonE4$ sides.

A trapezoid has $\maroonE4$ sides.

A hexagon has $6$ sides.

The following shapes are quadrilaterals:
 
 - Square
 - Trapezoid

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following shapes are rectangles?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
A $\maroonE{\text{rectangle}}$ is a polygon that *always* has:

 - $\maroonE{4}$ sides 
 - $\maroonE{2}$ pairs of parallel sides
 - $\maroonE{4 \text{ right}}$ angles
A heart is **not** a polygon, because it has $\blueE{\text{curved sides}}$.

A hexagon has $6$ sides.

A trapezoid has $\maroonE4$ sides but has $\goldE{\text{acute and obtuse angles}}$.

A square has $\maroonE4$ sides, $\maroonE{2}$ pairs of parallel sides and $\maroonE{4 \text{ right}}$ angles.

The following shape is a rectangle:
 
 - Square

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following shapes are polygons?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: A $\blueE{\text{polygon}}$ is a simple, closed figure with $\blueE{3 \text{ or more straight sides}}$.

A heart is **not** a polygon, because it has $\maroonE{\text{curved sides}}$.

A circle is **not** a polygon, because it has $\maroonE{\text{curved sides}}$.

So then, which of the shapes are polygons with $\blueE{3 \text{ or more straight sides}}$?  

A rectangle is a polygon with  $\blueE4$ sides. 

A hexagon is a polygon with  $\blueE{6}$ sides.

An octagon is a polygon with  $\blueE8$ sides.

The following shapes are polygons:

 - Rectangle
 - Hexagon 
 - Octagon

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which statements about $\blueE{\text{squares}}$ and $\maroonD{\text{rectangles}}$ are true?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
$\blueE{\text{Squares}}$ *always* have:

 - $\blueE{4}$ sides of $\blueE{\text{equal length}}$
 - $\blueE{2}$ pairs of parallel sides
 - $\blueE{4 \text{ right}}$ angles

[[☃ image 1]] [[☃ image 2]] [[☃ image 3]]

$\maroonD{\text{Rectangles}}$ *always* have:

 - $\maroonD{4}$ sides 
 - $\maroonD{2}$ pairs of parallel sides
 - $\maroonD{4 \text{ right}}$ angles

[[☃ image 1]] [[☃ image 2]] [[☃ image 3]]

$\maroonD{\text{Rectangles}}$ *sometimes*, but not always have:

- $4$ sides of equal length

$\blueE{\text{Squares}}$ and $\maroonD{\text{rectangles}}$ always have $4$ sides.  

All polygons with $4$ sides are quadrilaterals.

Both a $\blueE{\text{square}}$ and a $\maroonD{\text{rectangle}}$:

- always have $4$ right angles.
- are always quadrilaterals.

choices.content: Both shapes have all sides of equal length.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which statements about $\blueE{\text{squares}}$ and $\maroonD{\text{equilateral triangles}}$ are true?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
$\blueE{\text{Squares}}$ *always* have:

 - $\blueE{4}$ sides of $\blueE{\text{equal length}}$
 - $\blueE{2}$ pairs of parallel sides
 - $\blueE{4 \text{ right}}$ angles

[[☃ image 1]] [[☃ image 2]] [[☃ image 3]]

$\maroonD{\text{Equilateral triangles}}$ *always* have:

 - $\maroonD{3}$ sides of $\maroonD{\text{equal length}}$
 - $\maroonD{3}$ angles of $\maroonD{\text{equal measure}}$
 - $\maroonD{3 \text{ acute}}$ angles

[[☃ image 1]] [[☃ image 2]] [[☃ image 3]]

Both a $\blueE{\text{square}}$ and an $\maroonD{\text{equilateral triangle}}$:
 
- have all sides of equal length.
- have all angles of equal measure.

choices.content: Both shapes have all sides of equal length.
Both shapes have all angles of equal measure.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which statements about $\blueE{\text{rhombuses}}$ and $\maroonD{\text{rectangles}}$ are true?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
$\blueE{\text{Rhombuses}}$ *always* have:

 - $\blueE{4}$ sides of $\blueE{\text{equal length}}$
 - $\blueE{4}$ angles

[[☃ image 1]] [[☃ image 2]] [[☃ image 3]]

$\maroonD{\text{Rectangles}}$ *always* have:

 - $\maroonD{4}$ sides 
 - $\maroonD{2}$ pairs of parallel sides
 - $\maroonD{4 \text{ right}}$ angles

[[☃ image 1]] [[☃ image 2]] [[☃ image 3]]

$\maroonD{\text{Rectangles}}$ *sometimes*, but not always have:

- $4$ sides of equal length

$\blueE{\text{Rhombuses}}$ and $\maroonD{\text{rectangles}}$ always have $4$ sides.  

All polygons with $4$ sides are quadrilaterals.

Both a $\blueE{\text{rhombus}}$ and a $\maroonD{\text{rectangle}}$:

 - always have $4$ sides.
 - are always quadrilaterals.

choices.content: Both shapes have all sides of equal length.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which statements about $\blueE{\text{trapezoids}}$ and $\maroonD{\text{rhombuses}}$ are true?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
$\blueE{\text{Trapezoids}}$ *always* have:

* $\blueE{4}$ sides
* $\blueE{4}$ angles
 

[[☃ image 1]]  [[☃ image 2]]  [[☃ image 3]]

$\maroonD{\text{Rhombuses}}$ *always* have:

 - $\maroonD{4}$ sides of $\maroonD{\text{equal length}}$
 - $\maroonD{4}$ angles

[[☃ image 1]] [[☃ image 2]] [[☃ image 3]]

A $\blueE{\text{trapezoid}}$ and a $\maroonD{\text{rhombus}}$:

-  are $2$-dimensional figures.
- have the same number of  angles.

choices.content: Both shapes have the same number of angles.
Both shapes have all sides of equal length.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which statements about $\blueE{\text{pentagons}}$ and $\maroonD{\text{hexagons}}$ are true?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
$\blueE{\text{Pentagons}}$ *always* have:

- $\blueE{5 \text{ straight}}$ sides
- $\blueE{5}$ angles


[[☃ image 1]] [[☃ image 2]] [[☃ image 3]]

$\maroonD{\text{Hexagons}}$ *always* have:

- $\maroonD{6 \text{ straight}}$ sides
- $\maroonD{6}$ angles

[[☃ image 1]] [[☃ image 2]] [[☃ image 3]]
Both $\blueE{\text{pentagons}}$ and $\maroonD{\text{hexagons}}$ are closed figures with straight sides, so they are both polygons.

Both a $\blueE{\text{pentagon}}$ and a $\maroonD{\text{hexagon}}$:

- are polygons.
- have no curved sides.

choices.content: Both shapes have no curved sides.
Both shapes have the same number of sides.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which statement about $\blueE{\text{right triangles}}$ and $\maroonD{\text{hexagons}}$ are true?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
$\blueE{\text{Right triangles}}$ are $\blueE {\text{polygons}}$ with $\blueE{3}$ sides, $\blueE{1 \text{ right}}$ angle, and $\blueE{2 \text{ acute}}$ angles.

[[☃ image 1]] [[☃ image 2]] [[☃ image 3]]

$\maroonD{\text{Hexagons}}$ are $\maroonD {\text{polygons}}$ with $\maroonD{6}$ sides and $\maroonD{6}$ angles.

[[☃ image 1]] [[☃ image 2]] [[☃ image 3]]

Both a $\blueE{\text{right triangle}}$ and a $\maroonD{\text{hexagon}}$:

- are polygons.

choices.content: Both shapes have all sides of equal length.
Both shapes have the same number of sides.
